steps.title: Interpret 3% annual increase as a growth factor
steps.content:
A 3% increase means the population becomes 103% of the original, or 1.03 times the original.

So each year, the population is multiplied by the growth factor 1.03.

steps.title: Apply the exponential growth formula
steps.content:
For exponential growth with constant factor $k$ per unit time:

$$p(x) = P_0 \cdot k^x$$

Here, $P_0 = 2,000$ and $k = 1.03$:

$$p(x) = 2,000(1.03)^x$$

Verify at $x = 1$: $p(1) = 2,000 \cdot 1.03 = 2,060$, which is indeed 3% more than 2,000. That matches choice C — C is correct.
